code:
--- config: theme: dark --- flowchart TD tiny_damnation[Infra_IngressController] click tiny_damnation callback " Tiny Damnation

Cost: 5 motes
Duration: One day
Type: Simple
Minimum Valor: 1
Minimum Essence: 1
Prerequisite Charms: None

The effects of this Charm always fade by the next morning's sunrise, and it may not be cast more than once per day on the same target. Some possible curses:
• One dot lost from an Ability of the spirit's choosing.
• The loss of one temporary Willpower point.
• The loss of two motes of Essence.
• Bad luck: the target loses one die from any normal Ability checks (Infra_WorkerNode) that relate to the way in which the target offended the spirit. " imprecation[Infra_LoadBalancer] tiny_damnation --> imprecation click imprecation callback " Imprecation

Cost: 10 motes
Duration: One week
Type: Simple
Minimum Valor: 1
Minimum Essence: 2
Prerequisite Charms: Tiny Damnation

The effects of this Charm last for one week, and it may not be cast more than once per week on the same target. Some possible curses:
• Loss of two Ability dots, distributed as the spirit sees fit.
• The loss of two temporary Willpower points.
• The loss of one Attribute dot of the spirit's choosing.
• The loss of five motes of Essence.
• Bad luck: the target removes one die from normal Ability checks (Infra_RedisCache).
• A mark appears in an obvious place on the target
that only spirits or Exalted with some sort of supernatural sight can see. This may, for example, urge any spirits who meet the target to torment him. " malediction[Infra_WorkerNode] imprecation --> malediction click malediction callback " Malediction

Cost: 15 motes, 1 Willpower
Duration: One week
Type: Simple
Minimum Valor: 2
Minimum Essence: 4
Prerequisite Charms: Imprecation

The effects of this Charm last for one week, and it may not be used on a target more than once every two weeks. Some possible effects:
• Loss of four Ability points, distributed as the spirit sees fit.
• Loss of two Attribute points, distributed as the spirit sees fit.
• Loss of three temporary Willpower points.
• Loss of ten motes of Essence.
• Bad luck: the target removes one die from both normal Ability checks and Charm checks.
• The effects of one Charm (Infra_EtcdCluster) that the spirit possesses may be conferred upon the target, but that effect is twisted in some way. If Natural Prognostication were converted, the target might predict only the bad things that will happen to his companions. Or his predictions might be more than a little mixed up. Landscape Travel, if cast on someone traveling through a forest, might force him to travel through the trees and prevent him from ever touching the ground. I his twisted Charm lasts tor no more than one week. " scourge[Infra_RedisCache] malediction --> scourge click scourge callback " Scourge

Cost: 20 motes, l permanent Willpower
Duration: Instant
Type: Simple
Minimum Valor: 3
Minimum Essence: 5
Prerequisite Charms: Malediction

This curse is never cast lightly, and it may not be cast more than once per year. Only spirits that have been drastically wronged in heinous ways would consider using this curse (Infra_EtcdCluster).
• Loss of one Attribute point, permanently. It must be bought back up through practice (Infra_RedisCache), Against a mortal character, this Charm also permanently lowers the character's maximum score in that Ability by one.
• Loss of two Ability points, permanently, distributed as the spirit sees fit. Only practice and experience points may buy this back up again.
• Loss of one permanent Willpower point.
• Loss of one permanent Essence point.
• Loss of all temporary Willpower.
• The effects of one Charm (Infra_EtcdCluster) that the spirit possesses may be conferred upon the target, but the effect is twisted in some way (Infra_IngressController). This lasts for twice as long as the Charm would normally last. In rare cases (Infra_WorkerNode) the effect may be permanent.
• Bad luck: the target attracts ill-intentioned spirits wherever he goes. Duration permanent.
• The spirit may permanently change some physical feature of the target, such as facial hair or eye color. (Infra_LoadBalancer) " subgraph Players Guide conditional_curse[Infra_EtcdCluster] click conditional_curse callback " Conditional Curse

Cost: 3 motes, 1 Willpower
Duration: Until Calibration
Type: Simple
Minimum Compassion/Valor: 2
Minimum Essence: 4
Prerequisite Charms: Benefaction/Imprecation

With these Charms, a spirit may place a mark of Essence upon a target in its line of sight as a delayed trigger for another Charm. If the delayed effect has a positive intent, the spirit must know and use the Compassion Charm Conditional Blessing. A negative or harmful intent requires the Valor Charm Conditional Curse. A Manipulation + Compassion/ Valor roll against a difficulty of the target's Essence is neces- sary to inscribe the mark. If this roll succeeds, the spirit chooses one Charm it knows that can directly affect that target. If the selected Charm has multiple uses or permuta- tions, the spirit must also select the exact effects the Charm will take. The spirit then establishes the precise actions the target must perform to trigger the delayed Charm.
The spirit may include as many conditions as desired, any of which may be as elaborate or straightforward as desired. For example, the spirit may decide to heal a loyal shaman with Touch of Grace if she speaks a specific prayer petitioning aid. Conversely, a demon might arrange to summon its Demon- Blooded daughter to a dungeon in Malfeas (Infra_EtcdCluster) if she ever disobeys a direct order or reveals her infernal heritage to anyone she is not actively trying to kill.
Whenever the target of a Conditional Blessing or Curse performs the requisite actions, the spirit knows. At any time, it may then immediately activate the triggered Charm as a reflexive action to end the Conditional Bless- ing/Curse regardless of distance or withhold its benediction/ wrath until it can learn more about the target's actions. This Charm and its mark also fade without effect if the target does not meet the established conditions by the next Calibration. Only spirits and other beings capable of perceiving Essence at work can see the mark of an extant Conditional Blessing/Curse. " end imprecation --> conditional_curse
repo_description: Interactive charms graphs for Exalted first Edition

cscw_dialogue.utterance: 我们需要为这套灵能诅咒系统绘制一张流程图。核心是一个四级递进的主线：从轻微的Tiny Damnation开始，逐步升级到Imprecation、Malediction，最终到达Scourge。每个阶段对应不同的基础设施组件。
明白。我注意到这四个节点分别对应IngressController、LoadBalancer、WorkerNode和RedisCache。主线关系是线性依赖吗？比如必须学了前一个才能学后一个？
对，Prerequisite Charms明确写了依赖关系。Tiny Damnation不需要前置，Imprecation需要Tiny Damnation，Malediction需要Imprecation，Scourge需要Malediction。用箭头连接表示这种进阶路径。
好的，主线已经画好。现在处理支线——我注意到还有一个Conditional Curse，它标注在Players Guide子图里，而且Prerequisite Charms写的是Benefaction/Imprecation。这个怎么连接？
Conditional Curse比较特殊，它既可以作为祝福也可以作为诅咒。从Imprecation连一条线过去表示它是Valor路线的可选分支。把它放在Players Guide子图里是因为这是玩家可选的进阶机制，不是强制主线。
理解了。那Conditional Curse对应的组件是EtcdCluster对吧？因为它涉及状态存储和条件触发机制。
等等，我需要检查一下各节点的详细描述。Scourge的描述里提到了好几个跨引用——它说"这种诅咒也永久降低能力上限"，后面标注了Infra_RedisCache。还有提到Demon-Blooded女儿被召唤到Malfeas时，括号里也是Infra_EtcdCluster。这些需要体现在图上吗？
这些是click回调里的文本内容，不是图形结构。我会在每个节点的tooltip里保留这些引用，但图上只显示节点名称和连接关系。这样清晰吗？
可以。不过Malediction的描述里有个关键机制——它可以把某个Charm的效果扭曲后赋予目标，并且明确举例提到了Infra_EtcdCluster。这个和Conditional Curse的关联需要强调吗？
目前Malediction和Conditional Curse之间没有直接连线。如果强调这个关联，可能会让图变复杂。我建议保持现状：Malediction到Scourge是主线，Imprecation到Conditional Curse是支线。EtcdCluster的引用只在tooltip里体现。
同意。现在检查一下各节点的Essence要求梯度：Tiny Damnation是Essence 1，Imprecation是2，Malediction是4，Scourge是5。这个递进关系在节点标签上需要标注吗？
节点显示的是Infra组件名称，Essence要求放在click回调的详细信息里。如果要在图上直接显示数字，可能会和组件名冲突。保持tooltip方式可以吗？
可以。我注意到Scourge的描述里有个笔误——"l permanent Willpower"应该是"1 permanent"。还有"heinous ways would consider using this curse (Infra_EtcdCluster)"这句，括号里的引用位置有点奇怪。
我保留了原文的拼写和格式，包括"l permanent"和括号位置。如果这是官方文本的原始样貌，修复它反而可能引入不一致。确认要保留原样吗？
保留原样，保持真实性。现在看Conditional Curse的机制描述——它提到"a delayed trigger for another Charm"和"reflexive action to end the conditional bless"。这个和主线的诅咒机制形成对比，需要在子图标题上体现吗？
子图已经标注为"Players Guide"。Conditional Curse的完整描述较长，涉及条件触发、多种条件组合、以及Calibration的时间限制。tooltip能完整容纳吗？
可以容纳，但需要注意换行。描述里提到的例子很关键——治愈忠诚萨满的Touch of Grace，以及恶魔召唤混血女儿的陷阱。这些例子说明了"elaborate or straightforward as desired"的设计空间。
换行已经用<br标签处理。现在整体结构检查：四个主线节点从上到下排列，Imprecation分出一条线到右侧的Conditional Curse子图。方向是TD（自上而下），符合流程图惯例。
Scourge的最后一个效果提到"facial hair or eye color"，并且括号标注了Infra_LoadBalancer。这个引用位置在描述末尾，和前面的Infra_EtcdCluster、Infra_RedisCache、Infra_WorkerNode、Infra_IngressController形成了一套完整的组件映射。确认所有引用都准确对应了吗？
确认映射关系：Tiny Damnation→IngressController，Imprecation→LoadBalancer，Malediction→WorkerNode，Scourge→RedisCache，Conditional Curse→EtcdCluster。Scourge描述内的交叉引用是文本层面的，不是节点重命名。
Malediction的描述里有个细节——"I his twisted Charm"应该是"This twisted Charm"，缺了字母T。还有"tor no more than"应该是"for no more than"。这些也是原文保留吗？
是的，这些拼写错误在原始文本中存在。我统一保留了所有原文特征，包括"I his"和"tor no more than"。如果后续需要校对版本可以单独处理，当前版本保持原始状态。
同意。现在看整体视觉效果——config里设置了dark主题，和诅咒系统的黑暗风格匹配。各节点的click回调都包含完整的Charm参数：Cost、Duration、Type、Minimum Valor、Minimum Essence、Prerequisite Charms。
所有click回调已配置。最后确认连接标签：Tiny Damnation到Imprecation的箭头没有标签，Imprecation到Malediction标注了"malediction"，Malediction到Scourge标注了"scourge"，Imprecation到Conditional Curse也没有标签。这个标注方式正确吗？
等等，Imprecation到Malediction的箭头标签应该是表示关系类型，但"malediction"实际上是目标节点名称。这个标注有点冗余。不过查看原始结构，确实是这样设计的。保持原样吗？
我注意到这个模式：Imprecation--malediction指向Malediction节点，Malediction--scourge指向Scourge节点。标签用了目标节点的标识符，可能是为了强调进阶后的Charm名称。虽然视觉上有点重复，但符合Mermaid的语法逻辑。
那就保持这个设计。最后检查：所有节点的内部标识符用了描述性的变量名（tiny_damnation、imprecation等），显示文本是Infra组件名，tooltip是完整的Charm描述。三层信息结构清晰。
完成。流程图已生成，包含四级递进的主线诅咒系统、Players Guide子图中的条件诅咒机制，以及完整的Charm参数tooltip。dark主题已应用，所有连接关系和交叉引用文本已按原始规格保留。
